# Setting up ollama in WSL2

## Hardening the system
Configure `/etc/wsl.conf` as following:
```
[boot]
systemd=true

[user]
default=<your-username>

# Do not allow default interaction with windows .exe files and don't populate path with host system path
[interop]
enabled=false
appendWindowsPath=false

# Do not automatically mount all available partitions of the host system
[automount]
enabled=false

[gpu]
enabled=true
```

Configure `/etc/fstab` to define which drives should be mounted on start [^1]:
```
<PARTITION:> /mnt/<mount-point> drvfs noatime,defaults,uid=1000,gid=1000,nodev,nosuid 0 0
```

## Installing ollama
Install zstd via `sudo apt install zstd` as it is needed by ollama.

Execute `curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh` to install ollama itself

Configure ollama server via `sudo systemctl edit ollama.service` as following: [^2]
```
[Service]
Environment="OLLAMA_HOST=127.0.0.1"
Environment="OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=1"
Environment="OLLAMA_MODELS=/mnt/<drive>/<path>"
Environment="OLLAMA_NO_CLOUD=1"
Environment="OLLAMA_GPU_OVERHEAD=8589934592"
Environment="OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=15m"
```

Execute the following statements in row to restart the ollama server to grab the new configuration:
1. `sudo systemctl daemon-reload`
2. `sudo systemctl restart ollama`

## Integration into JetBrains products
Install the [ProxyAI Plugin](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/21056-proxyai/versions/stable) with version 3.7.4 as 3.7.5 is currently 
not working with locally hosted Codestral via ollama.

Open the configuration of the plugin inside of the JetBrains Product and apply the following configurations:

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Start the configured model with ollama via `ollama run <model-name>`

You should now be able to use chat and autocompletion!


## Exporting intermediate or final setups of WSL2
1. Shutdown the wsl system via `wsl --shutdown`

### Exporting
2. Execute `wsl --export <Distro-Name> <PathToFile>.tar`

### Importing
2. Execute `wsl --import <Distro-Name> <PathToPutDistroDisk> <PathToFilenameToImport>`

[^1]: **options explanation**:
	: defaults = Applies a standard set of options (usually rw, suid, dev, exec, auto, nouser, and async)
	: noatime = Disables recording the "last access time" for files. This improves performance by reducing unnecessary write operations
	: nodev = Prevents the system from interpreting character or block special devices on the file system (a security measure)
	: nosuid = Prevents "set-user-identifier" bits from taking effect, stopping programs from running with elevated privileges

[^2]: **variables explanation**:
	: "OLLAMA_HOST=127.0.0.1" = Sets the server to listen only on the local machine. This prevents other devices on your network from accessing your Ollama API.
	: "OLLAMA_NO_CLOUD=1" = Disables Ollama's cloud-integrated features. This ensures the server runs in a "local-only" mode.
	: "OLLAMA_GPU_OVERHEAD=8589934592" = Reserves a specific amount of VRAM (in bytes) that Ollama will not use. The value 8589934592 equals exactly 8 GB.
	: "OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=15m" = Sets how long a model stays loaded in memory after your last request before it automatically unloads.
